content: Graph neural networks (GNNs) can be repurposed to structure the sparse, incremental novel state representations that an ICM-curious autonomous agent encounters during open-ended exploration, turning unorganized exploration into a navigable relational map of under-explored state space. This allows the agent to avoid repeatedly re-exploring trivial, low-information novelty and prioritize high-impact, structurally disconnected regions of state space that align with long-term open-ended task discovery.
